codeReview.summary: Dashboard component is well-implemented with comprehensive financial summary display, per-item breakdown, and robust test coverage. Component follows React best practices with useMemo for performance, proper prop validation with defaults, and clear separation of concerns. All 23 tests pass and cover the key scenarios including empty states, calculations, coverage scenarios, and edge cases.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Dashboard component correctly uses the established pattern of co-locating test files with source files (Dashboard.test.jsx next to Dashboard.jsx), following the project's testing conventions documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/Dashboard/Dashboard.jsx and Dashboard.test.jsx are in the same directory, matching the convention stated in AGENTS.md: 'Tests: Co-located test files *.test.jsx or *.test.js in same directory as source'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the established pattern of using the @/ path alias for src/ imports, as documented in AGENTS.md Coding Conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Dashboard.jsx uses @/utils/valueCalculator.js import, consistent with AGENTS.md: 'Imports: Use @/ path alias for src/ imports'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the frontend-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote tests first (TDD RED), implemented (GREEN), verified, and committed. The handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure is not explicitly stated but evidence shows adherence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 'TDD: wrote 23 comprehensive tests covering empty state, calculations, all coverage scenarios, rapid updates — all passing' and verification includes both test run and manual browser verification

sharedStateObservations.observation: Dashboard correctly integrates with valueCalculator utilities and coverageLookup for calculations, showing proper understanding of the existing utility layer architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Dashboard.jsx imports from @/utils/valueCalculator.js and uses calculateValues, formatCurrency, formatPercentage, getUpgradeRecommendations functions as designed in the coverage-rules-engine feature

summary: Dashboard component successfully implements all required features: Total Detected Asset Value calculation, Protected Value display, prominent UNPROTECTED VALUE (big red number at 150%+ font size), Coverage Gap percentage, per-item breakdown with color-coded status badges, empty state messaging, upgrade recommendations, and positive message when all items are covered. The component handles edge cases gracefully (null/undefined inputs, rapid updates) and uses useMemo for performance optimization. All 23 tests pass, covering empty states, calculations, full/no/mixed coverage scenarios, rapid updates, policy changes, and responsive styling. Component is properly integrated into App.jsx and follows all project conventions.
